source_note: Readiness model is derived from Inquire-vector-search architecture, metrics, checkpointing, DLQ, and benchmark capabilities plus demo-specific target operating assumptions. It is not a raw runtime export.

control_plane.title: Managed ingestion control plane
control_plane.summary: Replace presenter-driven shell execution with persisted ingestion runs, batch manifests, operator actions, and promotion gates.

collection_management.title: Versioned collections and aliases
collection_management.promotion_policy: Ingest into a candidate collection, validate point count, smoke search, quality gates, and latency SLOs, then atomically promote the alias.
collection_management.rollback_policy: Keep the previous promoted version and move the alias back if quality, latency, or data-governance checks fail after promotion.
required_gates: collection point count increased as expected
seed manifest matches ingested object keys
p95 search latency remains under SLO
license and source metadata are present

observability.title: First-class observability
dashboards: Qdrant collection size and query latency
embedding provider latency and error rate
cache hit ratio and cache size
cost per batch and projected monthly cost

quality_loop.title: Search quality loop
workflow: run golden query suite against current and candidate collections
compare relevance and latency before promotion
block promotion on quality or latency regression
store benchmark artifact with run manifest
surface researcher feedback as future judgments

resilience_scale.title: Resilience and scalability
patterns: durable queue between source discovery and image workers
idempotent upserts keyed by source image identity and model version
checkpointed batch progress with resumable runs
dead-letter queue replay with operator approval
rate limits and backpressure around embedding providers
autoscaled Ray or Databricks worker pools
separate read and ingest capacity for Qdrant
backup and restore for object storage, vectors, manifests, and benchmark history
